programme.title: Mountain Monsters
programme.sub-title: The Mystery of the Death Cat Barn
programme.desc: During their quest to find the Cherokee Death Cat, the hunters heard a roar from a mysterious barn. Now as they investigate, they are led to a shocking discovery

programme.title: My Cat from Hell
programme.desc: Behaviourist Jackson Galaxy investigates the felines of Ryann and Derek, who do not get along - Buddy has resorted to being a full time bully to the more placid Lita. programme.title: Fish or Die
programme.desc: The intrepid anglers are in Borneo searching for the mahseer - a fish close to extinction. To find it, they risk entering the territory of the headhunting Dayak people

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: Baseball star Pete Rose wants Riverfront Stadium transformed into an aquarium, and Wayde and Heather travel to Horry County to build a huge tank

programme.title: Bridget Riley: Painting the Line
programme.desc: Bridget Riley has been challenging perception through painting for more than 60 years, with radical work that has transformed how many look at art. This documentary, featuring behind-the-scenes access and an interview with Kirsty Wark, tells the story of a true visionary of British art

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson reveals how some of the nations best-loved villages played a vital part in significant moments of history. He begins in Bamburgh in Northumberland, explaining how the health and fortunes of the village were shaped by those in charge of its famous castle, from early English kings to 18th-century philanthropists, who pioneered a welfare system nearly 200 years before one existed nationally. Ben visits fellow archaeologist Jessica Turner, whose findings show the seventh-century residents of Bamburgh were robust and healthy - contrary to the usual malnourishment of the British medieval population

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter embarks on a two-day walk along the Worcester and Birmingham Canal, which cuts a 30-mile path to the River Severn, discovering how it helped to generate wealth in the Industrial Revolution. Along the way, she follows a two-mile flight of 30 locks that lower the canal 220ft

programme.title: The Secret Rules of Modern Living: Algorithms
programme.desc: Without people noticing, modern life has been taken over. Algorithms run everything, from search engines on the internet, to satnavs and credit-card security - they even help people travel the world, find love and save lives. Here, mathematician Marcus du Sautoy demystifies this hidden world by showing some of the most essential algorithms. He reveals where these 2,000-year-old problem-solvers came from, showing how they work, what they have achieved and how they are now so advanced they can even program themselves

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: In the early 19th century Staithes was as one of the largest east coast fishing ports, and even now the harbour building renovations reveal the seaside villages impressive financial boom. Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the harsh North Yorkshire coastline that surrounds Staithes, as he finds out how the village was built to be a safe haven. It was also a settlement rich in the natural resource of shale. Contributors include local author Peter Appleton and fellow archaeologist Caroline Barrie Smith

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter takes a 20-mile walk along the Kennet and Avon Canal, learning about the history of the waterway, which provided a connection between the countrys two most important ports, Bristol and London. She also examines how the canal was restored to its former glory with lottery funds, before concluding her journey at the Caen Hill flight of locks

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell narrates this Timeshift documentary telling the story of how Britains phone network was built over the course of 100 years, from early call boxes to the Post Office Tower, and its impact on the public

programme.title: Children of the Tsunami
programme.desc: Exploring Japans 2011 tsunami through the eyes of children who witnessed the disaster. The programme features testimonies from youngsters at two schools - one where 74 pupils were killed by the giant wave, and the other close to the Fukushima power plant, where vital cooling systems were knocked out, triggering radiation leaks and forcing the evacuation of thousands of people

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: Yosser battles to hold his family together following his wifes departure, but with an increasingly fragile mental state and the police and social services closing in, he faces an uphill struggle to hang on to his children. Bernard Hill stars, with cameo appearances by Graeme Souness and Sammy Lee, two of the star players in the Liverpool side of the time

programme.title: Boys from the Blackstuff
programme.desc: As George casts his eye over the abandoned industrial infrastructure of the city, he reminisces about his politically active youth. Alan Bleasdales acclaimed 1980s drama, starring Peter Kerrigan, Michael Angelis, Bernard Hill and Alan Igbon

programme.title: Light and Dark
programme.desc: Part two of two. Professor Jim Al-Khalili visits the Gran Sasso National Laboratory in Italy, where scientists are attempting to capture and study dark matter. He also reveals how the discovery that the expansion of the universe is accelerating has led to theories that a mysterious new force called dark energy is in the process of gradually tearing the universe apart

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: In the early 19th century Staithes was as one of the largest east coast fishing ports, and even now the harbour building renovations reveal the seaside villages impressive financial boom. Archaeologist Ben Robinson explores the harsh North Yorkshire coastline that surrounds Staithes, as he finds out how the village was built to be a safe haven. It was also a settlement rich in the natural resource of shale. Contributors include local author Peter Appleton and fellow archaeologist Caroline Barrie Smith

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter takes a 20-mile walk along the Kennet and Avon Canal, learning about the history of the waterway, which provided a connection between the countrys two most important ports, Bristol and London. She also examines how the canal was restored to its former glory with lottery funds, before concluding her journey at the Caen Hill flight of locks

programme.title: Timeshift
programme.desc: Victoria Coren Mitchell narrates this Timeshift documentary telling the story of how Britains phone network was built over the course of 100 years, from early call boxes to the Post Office Tower, and its impact on the public

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Thorpeness in Suffolk, the first purpose-built seaside village in the UK. The ingenuity of amenities such as the hand-dug lake, country club and inland housing were the unique vision of Glencairn Stuart Ogilvie. Hamish Ogilvie helps Ben to understand his great-grandfathers vision, stemming from theatrical inspiration

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter travels to north Wales where, more than 200 years ago, engineer Thomas Telford created a 126ft-high aqueduct that spans the vale of Llangollen. She finds out why it has become a World Heritage Site by taking a six-mile walk along the winding Dee Valley, finishing at the landmark Telford described as a stream through the skies

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. The ruler also commissioned the Domesday Book to survey the wealth of the land and raise revenue, and set in motion the linguistic and cultural transformation of England. The Normans blended well in Scotland, but faced fierce opposition in Wales, and promoted divisions in Ireland which some claim still exist to this day

programme.title: Platoon (1986)
programme.desc: A raw recruit is thrown into the thick of the Vietnam War along the Cambodian border, where he finds himself caught up in a lethal feud between two sergeants. Oliver Stones Oscar-winning drama, the first of his Vietnam trilogy, starring Charlie Sheen, Willem Dafoe, Tom Berenger, Forest Whitaker, Kevin Dillon, Johnny Depp and John C McGinley

programme.title: Oliver Stone: Face to Face
programme.desc: Jeremy Isaacs talks to the writer and director Oliver Stone about his life and movies, which at the time of recording included Platoon, Born On the Fourth of July and Wall Street. First broadcast in September 1990

programme.title: The Bridge at Remagen (1969)
programme.desc: As Allied forces prepare for a major assault on Germany, a small unit of American soldiers is sent to capture a strategically important bridge over the Rhine - but German forces are determined to prevent the Allied advance, and seek to destroy it. Second World War adventure, starring George Segal, Robert Vaughn, Ben Gazzara and Bradford Dillman

programme.title: Villages by the Sea
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson uncovers the secrets of Thorpeness in Suffolk, the first purpose-built seaside village in the UK. The ingenuity of amenities such as the hand-dug lake, country club and inland housing were the unique vision of Glencairn Stuart Ogilvie. Hamish Ogilvie helps Ben to understand his great-grandfathers vision, stemming from theatrical inspiration

programme.title: Canal Walks with Julia Bradbury
programme.desc: The presenter travels to north Wales where, more than 200 years ago, engineer Thomas Telford created a 126ft-high aqueduct that spans the vale of Llangollen. She finds out why it has become a World Heritage Site by taking a six-mile walk along the winding Dee Valley, finishing at the landmark Telford described as a stream through the skies

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. The ruler also commissioned the Domesday Book to survey the wealth of the land and raise revenue, and set in motion the linguistic and cultural transformation of England. The Normans blended well in Scotland, but faced fierce opposition in Wales, and promoted divisions in Ireland which some claim still exist to this day

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong hosts as the satirical current affairs quiz returns for its 69th run. Team captains Ian Hislop and Paul Merton are joined by broadcaster Steph McGovern and comedian Phil Wang as guest panellists

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: The latest national and international stories as they break

programme.title: Morning Live

programme.title: Crimewatch Live
programme.desc: Essex Police need urgent help to find a suspect of a gruesome murder. Plus, how a gang brazenly stealing high-value cars was finally caught. With Rav Wilding and Michelle Ackerley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Three sets of developers who have all appeared on the show before tackle a variety of projects in Shropshire, County Durham and Staffordshire. Will their experience shine through, or are they destined to repeat mistakes of the past?

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Two teams in Ardingly, West Sussex, scour an antiques fair and pick up bargains to sell at auction. Host Charlie Ross also learns about the science of thermoluminescence

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather

programme.title: BBC News at One; Weather
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Two members of a throuple attempt to work out their issues in the wild, taking on a raft-building task and an abseil and climbing challenge that pushes their communication to the limit. In a campfire chat, they find a way to explain their fears with the help of relationship therapist Simone. Will three people prove too many for this marriage to cope with?

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr consider the kitchen. With a love for eating and entertaining, its crucial to the project that they get this social space perfect. They plan a Spanish-tiled extravaganza, but are initially unable to agree on a colour scheme. Alans sloppy bricklaying skills are put to the test when Scott ropes him in to try to build a traditional cooker hood, but Amanda is unimpressed with the result. The best friends head to Seville for a tapas tour at the citys gastronomic hub. Though not a fan of heights, Alan prepares to face his fears when invited on a rock-climbing course

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A career change means a couple urgently need Alistair Appletons help in finding them a rural property in the Derbyshire or Staffordshire countryside. While in the area - home to the Peak District National Park - Alistair turns his attention from property to scenery when he meets up with a group of keen countryside walkers

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk Rich join local craftsmen in in Kirkcudbright, Dumfries and Galloway, to restore a memorial sculpture scarred by decades of coastal weather in time for a remembrance service for lives lost at sea. In Kincardine, Fife, Will collects Lyndseys unusual family heirloom, a cast iron pan that cooked thousands of meals, hoping electrolysis will transform it from garden gate-stop to a camping essential, before heading to the Scottish Borders to learn from some celebrated rush-seated chair-makers

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Anne-Marie, Stephen, Savannah, Jan and Brandon take part in the game show, racing across the studio floor in moving podiums to try to win £5,000. Hosted by Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene

programme.title: Pointless

programme.desc: Clara Amfo and Vernon Kay present another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Priya is concerned for Ravis mental wellbeing, but Ravi heads to the gym to avoid her questioning. In the cafe, Ross tries to reassure Vicki of her safety, but their mood is soured when Mark arrives and chastises them both for getting involved. Peter supports Lauren as she prepares for her first day as manager of the car lot, but she is upset to learn that Max intends to run the business

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: Five of the remaining chefs compete in the real world at a pop-up restaurant in Boxhall food hall in Central London, with each of them coming up with a dish to impress 25 specially invited diners. The one judged most popular will send that chef straight through to the semi-finals. Back in the studio, the other hopefuls will have one last chance to stay in the running by cooking a family favourite - food that has meaning and is, above all, worthy of a place in the semi-final

programme.title: Ambulance
programme.desc: An insight into the work of frontline staff in Yorkshire. A major road closure means a category one patient is left waiting for help for over an hour, while frontline crew Sarah and Louise have no choice but to call for police backup when they are attacked by a patient suffering a seizure. A persistent and abusive caller causes major disruption for the team in the control centre, blocking the lines for those genuinely in need of help

programme.title: BBC News at Ten

programme.desc: Stephen Nolan probes the issues that matter to the people of Northern Ireland, focusing on the stories of the week and the people making the headlines in lively debates, with viewers sending in their questions via text, email and social media

programme.title: MOTD: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Action from the last-16 first leg ties, including Real Madrid v Manchester City at the Bernabeu and Paris Saint-Germain v Chelsea at Parc des Princes. Madrid knocked City out of this competition in the play-off round last term, but Pep Guardiolas men have already defeated them 2-1 here in the league phase this season. Holders PSG took on the Blues in a rematch of the FIFA World Club Championship final, which Chelsea won 3-0

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Quarrelling sisters Ayan and Hani come to Bear to help break their cycle of constant bickering and put-downs theyve been stuck in since childhood, but the city girls are in for a shock when it comes to surviving in the wild. The adventurer sets them a problem-solving challenge which they must tackle in a pouring rainstorm and a physical test of their resilience and teamwork

programme.title: Amanda and Alans Spanish Job
programme.desc: Amanda Holden and Alan Carr go all-out designing a Moorish-themed master bedroom. Amanda steers the ship in the bedroom whilst Alan takes charge in the adjoining lounge. However, creative tensions soon arise when Amanda wants to get rid of Alans favourite curved arch which links the spaces together. The DIY duo take some much-needed time out to explore the picturesque coastline of Nerja, but theres trouble at sea. When Alan decides to take a shortcut, he ends up capsizing their kayak

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse goes house hunting in Argyll and Bute, and meets an artist who takes his inspiration from the landscape and the wildlife that lives there

Will Kirk discovers the delicate craft of marionette making

programme.desc: Roman Kemp and Sarah Greene host the quiz in which Naomi, Matthew, John, Charlotte and Rohan race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000. The contenders battle across a series of fast-paced qualifiers and head-to-head races where the aim of the game is simple - to make it to the finish line before their rivals. The winner then races against the track itself in an attempt to win the cash prize

programme.title: Dragons Den
programme.desc: Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Steven Bartlett are joined by beauty mogul Jenna Meek as they grill the latest business hopefuls. Pitching to the Dragons this time - a founder from Northern Ireland presents her snack range designed to unleash the natural power of oats. A father-and-son duo unveil their lockdown-born DIY gadget. A software developer from London brings a real-world hide-and-seek adventure with a tech twist. And a physiologist from Lancashire hopes to turn heads with her protective, sun-shielding lip care range that doubles as everyday cosmetic skincare

programme.title: The Apprentice

But are they telling the truth, or are they making it all up?

programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The story of Kilmarnock nurse Adele Rennie, who pretended to be handsome, professional men to target, harass, stalk and exploit hundreds of women. When Dr David Graham starts flirting with former beauty queen Abbie Draper online, she turns sleuth and uncovers a trail of deception, crazy coincidences and stories that just dont add up

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Abbie Draper takes her findings to the police, but they are unable to do anything about it and the Catfish of Kilmarnock remains free to target more and more victims. However, Abbie cannot walk away and steps up her investigation over months that turn into years, with victims spread across the country and dating back over a decade

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: The victims finally have justice, but are still left urgently searching for answers. What drives someone, with a seemingly normal life to terrorise countless women from the same town? Those closest to the perpetrator, who are also trying to understand the mystery behind the repeated cycles of offending, share their thoughts

programme.title: Men of the Manosphere
programme.desc: James Blake enters the virtual world of manhood, often referred to as the manosphere, in search of status, wealth and relationships. He meets three young men who have turned to male social media influencers for guidance and instruction as they seek to better themselves and achieve success in different areas of their lives, both on and offline

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommys recovery is back on track after he and John put his hand to the test. The pair are training again, and the search for Tommys next opponent begins. Eager to make the most of family time, Tommy splashes out on a £90,000 motor home for family trips with Molly and Bambi and takes his mother out for lunch and a long-overdue catch-up. Tommy reveals that John has had a recent health scare, so to cheer him up, father and son take their very different caravans to a local steam fair

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall visits Thailand where he encounters a king cobra, the longest venomous snake in the world and meets two dangerous members of the cat family. He also scales a building in Bangkok to illustrate the abilities of one of wildlifes best climbers

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve Backshall learns about primates in Uganda, encountering gorillas in the mountains and baboons on the savannah, and watching chimps in a forest at dawn

programme.title: Dragons Den
programme.desc: Founder of Tropic Skincare, Susie Ma, joins Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Steven Bartlett in the den. The Dragons hear pitches for a nose-friendly home composter, a pre-loved kitchen business a new design for a medical bag and a DIY drinks infusion kit. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Lord Sugar sends the candidates to Egypt, tasking them with organising corporate away days for paying clients. While one team embarks on a desert adventure that ends up being a bumpy ride, the others head out on a lagoon tour which proves to be less than relaxing

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the Egypt task, the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Living Every Second: The Kris Hallenga Story
programme.desc: Diagnosed with Stage IV breast cancer at 23, Kris Hallenga made a radical decision about what she wants to do with the rest of her life. Together with her twin sister, Maren, Kris founded the Coppafeel charity, harnessing the power of her own experience to show millions of young people how to check for signs of cancer. In 2013, Kris made a film about her life for the BBC and, over the decade that followed, continued to invite the cameras back. This film reveals how her energy, drive and ambition has left an important legacy

programme.title: Kensukes Kingdom (2023)
programme.desc: A boy and his dog are washed overboard from their boat, leaving them stranded on a Pacific island. The youngster finds support from a fellow castaway, who helps him to protect the fragile home he has built for himself. Animated adventure based on Michael Morpurgos novel, featuring the voices of Aaron MacGregor, Sally Hawkins, Cillian Murphy, Raffey Cassidy and Ken Watanabe

programme.title: Men of the Manosphere
programme.desc: James Blake enters the virtual world of manhood, often referred to as the manosphere, in search of status, wealth and relationships. He meets three young men who have turned to male social media influencers for guidance and instruction as they seek to better themselves and achieve success in different areas of their lives, both on and offline

programme.title: EastEnders Investigates: The Manosphere
programme.desc: An investigation into the issues of misogyny and toxic masculinity, to coincide with the current EastEnders storyline centred on teenager Joel Marshall, played by Max Murray

programme.title: My Mates a Bad Date
programme.desc: Twenty-one-year-old Khalil from Nottingham cannot get a second date. Oloni thinks that remembering his dates names, dropping the baby girl tag and cooling off on the bragging might help his chances

programme.title: RuPauls Drag Race UK vs the World
programme.desc: This week, the makeover challenge goes Olympic as the queens drag up a host of Team GB Olympians, including Greg Rutherford, Shanaze Reade, Molly Thompson-Smith, Celia Quansah and Daniel Jervis. Pop superstar Anastacia joins RuPaul on the judging panel alongside Michelle Visage and Graham Norton

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the days news

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Part two of two. Steve Backshall continues his adventure in the Philippines, where he swims with one of the deadliest snakes on the planet, meets a shark that spends most of its time more than 500 metres underwater, and searches in the jungle for a tarsier - a predator that eats its victims head-first

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Part one of two. Steve Backshall visits the Philippines, where he encounters creatures including an armoured predator and an arachnid with legs four times longer than its body

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mails delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: Louis immerses himself in Floridas multimillion-dollar rap scene, which is as notable for the controversy surrounding its emerging stars as its creativity. He spends time with a number of artists who are achieving success through self-promotion on social media, from those playing out fierce rivalries online to rappers live-streaming their often violent and chaotic lifestyles. While drawing in an ever-growing fanbase attracted to its unvarnished portrayal of real-life struggles and hardship, this content has also drawn the attention of the authorities, who see it as evidence of criminals indulging in illegal gang activity

programme.title: Road House (1989)
programme.desc: A philosophy-reading kung fu expert gains a reputation as the best bouncer around, and is hired by the owner of a rowdy Missouri bar to keep the peace. When he fires some of the establishments corrupt staff, he incurs the wrath of a local crime boss who has a stranglehold on the towns businesses. Action thriller, starring Patrick Swayze, Ben Gazzara, Kelly Lynch, Sam Elliott and blues singer Jeff Healey

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Its Tommys birthday, but theres no time for cake. With just days to go before his long-awaited comeback fight in Budapest, Tommy is pulled in every direction - juggling press conferences, promo shoots and the pressure of proving hes still got what it takes in the ring

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: After the success of his comeback fight, Tommys road to redemption hits a major setback - his hand is injured again and needs eight weeks total rest before doctors will know if he can fight again. The last time he couldnt train, his life spiralled out of control, so now Tommy throws himself into a frenzy of activity - a TV appearance on The Wheel, family days out with Bambi and a final push to finish the home he hopes Molly and Bambi will soon return to

programme.title: Louis Therouxs Forbidden America
programme.desc: Louis immerses himself in Floridas multimillion-dollar rap scene, which is as notable for the controversy surrounding its emerging stars as its creativity. He spends time with a number of artists who are achieving success through self-promotion on social media, from those playing out fierce rivalries online to rappers live-streaming their often violent and chaotic lifestyles. While drawing in an ever-growing fanbase attracted to its unvarnished portrayal of real-life struggles and hardship, this content has also drawn the attention of the authorities, who see it as evidence of criminals indulging in illegal gang activity

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Richard Hammond and James May test the Porsche Panamera against the Royal Mails delivery service, racing a first-class letter to see which travels faster over the full length of the British Isles. Jeremy Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their most deadly toys, and multi Olympic gold medal-winning sprinter Usain Bolt is the Star in a Reasonably Priced Car

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy realises just how far he and Molly have come since the separation, but Johns worries grow as Tommy takes on a 100km triathlon, pushing his body to new physical limits. Before the challenge, Tommy takes some downtime at the spa with his mates, preparing for a huge press push ahead of his series premiere. After completing a series of intense physical challenges, he has to deal with a social media backlash

programme.title: Tommy: The Good. The Bad. The Fury
programme.desc: Tommy receives news of the untimely death of boxing legend Ricky Hatton, which reminds him to live life to its fullest. He then sets out on the most gruelling test of endurance possible - climbing the three highest mountains in England, Scotland and Wales in just 24 hours. At Halloween, Tommy and John go pumpkin picking with Bambi, things are back on track with Molly, and the house renovation is almost completed

programme.title: Last Woman on Earth with Sara Pascoe
programme.desc: The comedian heads to Denmark, where she learns how to build from a Lego master, makes a roof out of seaweed with a group of thatchers, meets a maritime tattoo artist and finds out about sail-making

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Lord Sugar sends the candidates to Egypt, tasking them with organising corporate away days for paying clients. While one team embarks on a desert adventure that ends up being a bumpy ride, the others head out on a lagoon tour which proves to be less than relaxing

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the Egypt task, the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: Tensions run high as Jo and Freya meet for the first time in months. Both want to reconcile, but neither wants to lose ground. Meanwhile, at the behest of the Olivias, Abe has been roped into staging his and Freyas surprise proposal as the first big piece of content for their disability wedding and bridal influencer campaign. Starring Kyla Harris and Elena Samuel

programme.title: Marcus Wareing Simply Provence
programme.desc: As Marcuss time in Saint Remy nears its end, he is invited to a farm party - but he has to bring a dish. In need of inspiration for a celebratory, but simple, recipe, he calls on local farmer Sebastien, who supplies vegetable boxes for his customers, and visits a restaurant where he learns how to make a famous local artichoke dish

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: The presenter tries to resolve conflicts in the wilderness, uniting people with unresolved feuds for a bespoke challenge set by a team of experts that will force them to work together. Former best friends Rhys and Niall work to repair their broken friendship. They drifted apart after the death of Nialls older brother and have barely spoken for two years

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: In Birmingham, Will Kirk and Dominic Chinea turn a neglected phone box into a life-saving defibrillator station, while in west London, Will learns the historical and romantic art of fan-making with expert Victoria Ajoko. Back at the barn, Dom uncovers a pair of secateurs owned by a D-Day paratrooper

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: In Scotlands Central Lowlands, Sonali Shah helps a Canadian couple to make a rural escape and meets Californians who bought their dream home, sight unseen, and never looked back

programme.title: Big Cats 24/7
programme.desc: The Xudum prides mothers and young cubs flee south as four dangerous intruder male lions close in, threatening to kill cubs and take over the pride. For Pobe the cheetah, the pressure is on to provide for her family, but as one of the Okavangos underdogs, she must defend every meal from bigger cats. Leopard mum Lediba has no choice but to leave her cubs alone while she hunts, despite the sinister presence of an unknown male

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts a game show in which contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway heads to Llandudno to explore the rugged beauty of Great Orme, a giant headland overlooking the Irish Sea. Along the way he visits St Tudnos church and copper mines that date back 4,000 years

programme.title: New Zealand: Earths Mythical Islands
programme.desc: New Zealand is now home to some of the richest agricultural land in the world, boasting magnificent gardens with plants from around the planet and rare magical creatures. A caving expedition reveals relics of the original lost world of its pre-human past; the actual bones of a cow-sized flightless grazing bird, a moa, among the largest birds that has ever lived. However, as narrator Sam Neill reveals, against all the odds, one giant bird from primeval New Zealand has survived to this day

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Michael Aspel and the team pay a visit to the Valley Leisure Centre in Biddulph, Staffordshire. Items of interest include a rare 18th-century Wedgwood egg scrambler, a Constable sketch, a fine English repeater watch, a collection of ships documents giving details of the auctioning of slaves and a handkerchief with a royal connection

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Antiques experts James Braxton and Mark Stacey value collectibles in Bristol for people eager to make easy money at auction, while presenter Paul Martin finds out about collecting cigarette cards

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith face off, demonstrating the general knowledge and trivia skills that made them champions last time

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay and tour guide Paul Gorton test Owain Wyn Evans, Greg Rutherford and Abby Cook on their knowledge of Paisley, in the west central Lowlands of Scotland

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs representing central England serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. They include a duck breast and celeriac bread sauce celebrating the special effects team who worked on Inception, as well as an edible film reel with white chocolate and ginger mousse. Veteran judge Spencer Metzger helps to decide who deserves to stay and who should go. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Britains Biggest Warship
programme.desc: HMS Queen Elizabeth goes on dangerous sea trials for the first time in the North Sea, where every part of the vessel is put to the test and the first aircraft lands on the deck. After a few days at sea, the first problems start to emerge, including an explosion, a dangerous fuel leak, and some strange noises. Meanwhile, a crew member reveals how the Navy helped him make a fresh start from a life of crime, and the ships Muslim chef works out how to cook pork without offending his religion

programme.title: Hostage
programme.desc: The documentary investigates the immediate fallout from the kidnap of John Cantlie and James Foley, as a perilous manhunt was launched by an expert hostage negotiator. But as the country collapsed, Islamic State took hold - and as more journalists joined them in captivity, survival became a desperate struggle

programme.title: Spotlight
programme.desc: Marie-Louise Connolly investigates the difficult choice facing new mothers who experience serious mental health issues after giving birth. In Northern Ireland, treatment means being separated from their babies. This is the only part of the UK without a specialist mother and baby unit that would that allow in-patient treatment with their baby beside them

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events, presented by Nick Watt

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: We Might Regret This
programme.desc: A benefits assessor comes to evaluate Freyas needs and determine how many hours of PA financial assistance she will receive. Jo unexpectedly turns up at the venue for Janes book launch to try to make some progress with a clearly wounded Levi

programme.title: Unspun World with John Simpson
programme.desc: The BBCs world affairs editor reflects on the weeks major global news stories, talking to local experts from around the world to go behind the headlines

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Four chefs representing central England serve their canapes, starters and fish dishes inspired by the British film industry, with Michelin-starred chef Spencer Metzger as this weeks veteran judge. Highlights include a Jerusalem artichoke and turnip dish linked to War Horse and a monochrome, art-deco styled monkfish dish that pays homage to Birminghams Electric Theatre. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Europe on the Edge with Katya Adler
programme.desc: The journalist finds out what the future holds for Europe in a world where it can no longer fully rely on America, beginning by visiting Italy and Germany to explore how theyre changing in a turbulent new world. In the Tuscan hills, she joins Italian paratroopers on a Nato exercise with 1,500 soldiers from seven different countries, then she heads to Germany, where she visits a Volkswagen car factory to learn how theyre being affected by both Russia and China. And in Germanys far south, not far from Munich, she meets a family who are building a house with a nuclear bunker

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: In March 2018, a driverless car struck and killed a woman crossing the street in Tempe, Arizona, making it the worlds first pedestrian fatality from a car that was being driven by AI. But there was also a human operator behind the wheel, monitoring the vehicle, and a decision had to be made who was responsible, the human, or the AI? In her first TV interview, operator Rafaella Vasquez tells the story of the crash and its aftermath, while Hannah also talks to the lead detective on the case. She also tracks down a whistleblower from Uber, the company behind the driverless vehicle, and takes her first rides in autonomous vehicles in the US and UK

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Mum Rachel needs Bears to repair her bond with 21-year-old daughter Venice. Rachel is desperate to build a loving and respectful relationship between them - one she feels she missed out on with her own mother. As they embark on a kayaking task, it isnt long before emotions run high and tempers flare

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea and Will Kirk Rich join local craftsmen in in Kirkcudbright, Dumfries and Galloway, to restore a memorial sculpture scarred by decades of coastal weather in time for a remembrance service for lives lost at sea. In Kincardine, Fife, Will collects Lyndseys unusual family heirloom, a cast iron pan that cooked thousands of meals, hoping electrolysis will transform it from garden gate-stop to a camping essential, before heading to the Scottish Borders to learn from some celebrated rush-seated chair-makers

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson meets retiree Jenny who is moving from Kent to the North Wessex Downs to be closer to her son, Ollie, and is hoping her £300,000 budget will find her a rosy cottage to call home

programme.title: Gardeners World
programme.desc: Monty Don breathes new life into a tired agapanthus, pots up a fig tree for future harvests and shares a tip for growing roses. A retired GP shows Frances Tophill around his West Yorkshire garden, where prairie planting is the order of the day, Ashley Edwards explores the resilience of drought-resistant plants at RHS Hyde Hall and Arit Anderson visits the garden of two designers who have blended three distinct planting styles into one

programme.title: BBC News
programme.desc: A round-up of the latest headlines

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: The latest stories from Westminster and beyond

programme.title: Impossible
programme.desc: Rick Edwards hosts the game show in which 24 recurring contestants compete for the chance to win £10,000, with a single impossible answer knocking them out for the day

programme.title: Unbeatable
programme.desc: Over three rounds, contestants vie to find the single unbeatable answer and streak ahead of the rest to make the final where they can win a cash prize. General knowledge quiz, hosted by Jason Manford

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Susan Calman in which one contestant becomes the Boss and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which player is best suited to answer questions

programme.title: Weatherman Walking
programme.desc: Derek Brockway takes a circular walk around Carreg Cennen Castle, one of the most dramatic, photogenic and romantic strongholds in Wales

programme.title: Scotland from the Sky
programme.desc: James Crawford takes to the skies to explore Scotlands cities, coasts and countryside. He begins by examining the origins of aerial photography itself and then takes a birds-eye view of forgotten factories, abandoned villages and secret military installations

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The popular series visits Glamis Castle, childhood home of the Queen Mother and the place where Macbeth is said to have killed Duncan. The experts value a posy ring with a macabre history, a dining table originally intended for supporting a coffin, jewellery which survived not only a fire but a torpedo attack and the fascinating scrapbook of a First World War pilot. Presented by Michael Aspel

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Raj Bisram and David Harper assess items brought to Allerton Castle in North Yorkshire, while Paul Martin finds out about Harrogates connection with Russias last tsarina

programme.title: Richard Osmans House of Games
programme.desc: Former winners Chris McCausland, Claire Richards, Adele Roberts and Olly Smith test their general knowledge skills in the penultimate round of challenges

programme.title: Do You Know Your Place?
programme.desc: Vernon Kay returns for the penultimate day of this weeks quiz as the trip heads to Kirkby Lonsdale in Cumbria. With the leaderboard tightening, presenter Owain Wyn Evans, Olympic champion Greg Rutherford and Blue Peters Abby Cook are all vying for victory. Paul Gorton resumes his role as unreliable tour guide, scattering facts and fabrications as the competition edges closer to the finale

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two highest-scoring chefs from central England must go head-to-head and cook their six-course menus again. Tom Kerridge, Lorna McNee and Phil Wang are joined by acclaimed film producer, Bafta winner and Oscar nominee Alison Owen. Only one of the chefs will triumph and go through to represent central England at the national finals. Andi Oliver presents

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: In Arran, Callum has to gather their flock from 4,000 acres of steep, rough hill terrain, while Zara recalls the devastating effects of his quadbike accident on that same ground. In Dornoch, Rob and Emma need the vets help when a ewe gets into trouble birthing triplets, while Ally and daughter Bethany travel by boat to gather sheep on an uninhabited island to bring them home to their croft on Lewis

programme.title: Dunblane: How Britain Banned Handguns
programme.desc: On March 13, 1996, Thomas Hamilton walked into the gym hall in Dunblane Primary School in Stirlingshire and shot dead 16 pupils and their teacher Gwen Mayor, seriously injuring 14 other pupils and two teachers. This documentary follows the campaign to ban private handgun ownership in the UK in the wake of the massacre, revealing how a group of ordinary people forced the government to ensure the tragedy could not happen again

programme.title: The Apprentice: Unfinished Business
programme.desc: As Alan Sugar continues his search for a new business partner, Angela Scanlon and her guests OJ Borg and Karen Hauer take a deep dive into the candidates performances and analyse the latest task, and the drama of the boardroom. Plus, a chat with the latest person to leave the process

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the days events with Patrick OConnell

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: The latest forecast

programme.title: AI Confidential with Hannah Fry
programme.desc: The mathematician explores the link between a controversial AI algorithm and Luigi Mangione, the man accused of killing UnitedHealthcare CEO Brian Thompson. There were allegations the company used artificial intelligence to deny insurance claims from vulnerable patients, while Mangione himself studied AI at university. Hannah travels to the US to meet a woman who lost her husband and claims it happened after he was denied healthcare by an algorithm, and she also speaks to a UnitedHealthcare insider who reveals her concerns about their use of AI

programme.title: Jack the Ripper: The Case Reopened
programme.desc: Silent Witness star Emilia Fox and Professor of Criminology David Wilson re-examine the infamous murders, which took place in the Whitechapel district of London in the late 19th century. Using state-of-the-art technology, they hope to get closer than anyone has before to answering vital questions, including who Jack the Ripper was, what motivated his crimes, how he escaped justice, and whether he could have been responsible for even more brutal murders

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The remaining chefs representing central England serve up their mains and desserts inspired by the British film industry. They include a duck breast and celeriac bread sauce celebrating the special effects team who worked on Inception, as well as an edible film reel with white chocolate and ginger mousse. Veteran judge Spencer Metzger helps to decide who deserves to stay and who should go. Presented by Andi Oliver

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: Mervin stumbles across a crime scene when he goes in search of his brother Solomon on Antigua. In a remote cabin, a local businessman has been shot. But Solomon and his associate Cheech both swear the other is responsible, and theyre not letting Mervin go until each one has convinced him of their account

programme.title: The Walsh Sisters
programme.sub-title: Its Not the Worst Thing Thats Happened This Week
programme.desc: Anna is in the hospital, reeling from events of the incident and desperate for answers. In rehab, Rachel is restless and increasingly agitated, struggling through withdrawal. Meanwhile, Maggie tells Garv she wants to start IVF, but he brushes her off, reluctantly agreeing to a rigid sex schedule

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Bear Grylls: Wild Reckoning
programme.desc: Two members of a throuple attempt to work out their issues in the wild, taking on a raft-building task and an abseil and climbing challenge that pushes their communication to the limit. In a campfire chat, they find a way to explain their fears with the help of relationship therapist Simone. Will three people prove too many for this marriage to cope with?

programme.title: The Repair Shop on the Road
programme.desc: In South Yorkshire, Will Kirk and Cindy Welland help restore a 19th-century grandfather clock for Doncaster School for the Deaf and in Birmingham, Dominic Chinea brings a classic coffee machine back to life for a family honouring their fathers legacy. Will Kirk discovers the delicate craft of marionette making

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: Clive hits the road with a female Moroccan motorcycle gang and also visits the Juventus Academy to train with some of the countrys best young footballing talent. He goes antique shopping with an expert to haggle for some of Casablancas best bargains, and meets a Michelin-starred chef to grab the best morning catch to cook in his new fine-dining restaurant

programme.title: Clive Myries African Adventure
programme.desc: Clive concludes his journey by travelling to the northern tip of Morocco and meeting band the Master Musicians of Joujouka high in the Rif mountains. In a nearby farm, he grabs a bucket and helps milk goats to produce the smooth and creamy jben cheese. In the winding streets of Casablancas old town, he learns new skills from a lantern-making artisan and meets a local fashion influencer to find the perfect Moroccan kaftan.

programme.desc: Documentary highlighting what its producers see as undeniable evidence of alien contact, which is suggested can no longer be concealed by government. programme.desc: Musician Matt Willis and his TV presenter wife Emma team up with their family to take on actor Gary Lucy (The Bill) and his clan in the hope of winning the £30,000 charity jackpot. Vernon Kay asks the questions
